insights.title: Gender parity over time
insights.detail: Gender parity has improved by 0.09 points between 2015 and 2026.

insights.title: Platform diversity gap
insights.detail: Amazon Prime leads on diversity (0.73) while Apple Tv trails (0.69).

insights.title: Genre lead-role skew
insights.detail: Action has the highest male lead share at 74% of lead roles.

insights.title: Dialogue gap by genre
insights.detail: In documentary, female characters average 28% fewer dialogue words than male characters.

insights.title: Format-specific imbalance
insights.detail: Hbo Max is weakest in sports doc where gender parity is 0.40 and the sample still points to documentary dominance.

insights.title: Intersectional representation
insights.detail: Women of color hold 14% of lead roles across the dataset.

insights.title: Interaction homophily
insights.detail: Gender homophily in interaction networks is 0.04 (positive values mean characters interact mostly within their own group).

power_law.interpretation: View counts follow a power law with exponent 1.86: a winner-take-most regime where a few videos capture most of the attention.

inequality.interpretation: A Gini of 0.70 means attention is extremely concentrated - the top 3 channels alone hold 46% of all views.

rows.label: Channel reach (subscribers)
rows.help: How many subscribers the uploading channel has - a proxy for built-in reach.

rows.label: Duration (minutes)
rows.help: Track length in minutes. The data peaks near the 3-minute radio-single norm.

rows.label: Description length (characters)
rows.help: Character length of the video description.

rows.label: Official video
rows.help: 1 if the title/tags mark it an official video, else 0.

rows.label: Tag count
rows.help: How many YouTube tags the upload declared.

rows.label: Title length (words)
rows.help: Number of words in the video title.

rows.label: Title length (characters)
rows.help: Number of characters in the video title.

clusters.name: Supernovas
clusters.blurb: Explosive debuts from mega-channels - massive reach, instant escape velocity.

clusters.name: Established Giants
clusters.blurb: Big artists converting loyal fanbases into dependable nine-figure runs.

clusters.name: Viral Waves
clusters.blurb: Mid-reach uploads that punch far above their subscriber base - shareability wins.

clusters.name: Emerging Stars
clusters.blurb: Algorithm-discovered growth; strong views-per-subscriber on a smaller base.

clusters.name: Hidden Gems
clusters.blurb: Niche resonance and slow-burn acceleration from the smallest channels.

publication_timeline.note: Bars show dated rows only. Blank line segments mean the current public corpus does not expose a usable year for that point.

artist.title: Artist lens
artist.summary: This is a market-shape read, not a songwriting oracle. In the classified slice, Electronic/Dance carries the strongest share of visible attention, the strongest duration pocket is 3–4 min, and collaboration helps most when it expands reach without making the track anonymous.
artist.takeaway: The practical question is not which genre is supposedly 'best.' It is whether your release shape can still find oxygen once attention narrows around a few familiar patterns.

consumer.title: Consumer lens
consumer.summary: Listeners are moving through a field that is both concentrated and partially labeled. The visible publication window spans 2009 to 2025, but only 26% of rows carry public year data, which means breadth has to be read with some caution.
consumer.takeaway: A healthy discovery system should feel broader than its hit list. If the surface feels repetitive, the numbers suggest you may not be imagining it.

business.title: Business lens
business.summary: Attention remains narrow enough that a small set of channels can still steer the visible market. The top 5 hold 12.5% of views and the top 10 hold 19.1%, which is useful if you need to separate repeatable distribution logic from one-off mythology.
business.takeaway: The value is not 'which song went viral.' It is which conditions keep narrowing or opening the market across multiple cohorts.

research.title: Research lens
research.summary: The useful question is not whether a pattern exists somewhere, but whether it survives classification gaps, coverage limits, and cohort changes. This lane is strongest when it keeps the leaderboard separate from the broader structural context instead of pretending they are the same thing.
research.takeaway: The method earns trust when the story gets smaller where coverage is weak, and sharper where multiple checks agree.

bias.interpretation: Analysing 636 public songs across multiple datasets. Known-gender coverage is 21% and known-genre coverage is 15%, so the equity read should be treated as directional rather than total. Within the labeled subset, gender parity is 0.98 and female artists average 463,818,200 views/song versus 734,129,344 for male artists (ratio 0.63). Genre concentration is high overall (Gini 0.838), while the classified-only genre diversity reads 0.473. The top 5 channels still control 12.5% of all views.

core_top_100.purpose: Used for the core view, inequality, correlation, archetype, network, and prediction charts.

enriched_context.purpose: Used where broader structural checks matter: bias, language mix, publication timing, and resonance context.

source_audit.notes: Primary cohort used for the core charts and leaderboard surfaces.
Filtered to music videos before deduplication against the primary cohort.
Adds duration, engagement, subscriber, and publication timing context where available.

cleaning_steps: Coerce view counts to numeric and drop rows that still cannot be measured.
Normalize title strings before cross-source deduplication so the same song is not counted twice.
Convert durations to minutes and fill missing durations with the source median rather than inventing a default.
Fill missing subscriber counts with the source median so reach-adjusted comparisons remain stable.
Map channel countries only when a public lookup exists; otherwise keep the value as Unknown instead of guessing.
Carry publication years directly from public date fields, and only infer a year from free text when a single recent year or copyright marker is unambiguous.
Keep the top-100 cohort separate from the enriched context so broad structural claims do not overwrite the core leaderboard.
guardrails: Virality coefficient is excluded from the predictor set because it is derived from views and would leak the target.
All prediction scores are reported out-of-fold with 5-fold cross-validation, not in-sample fit.
Power-law confidence intervals use 500 bootstrap resamples over the fitted tail only.
Correlation rows include partial Spearman checks so raw associations are not mistaken for independent effects.
Publication timelines are shown only for rows with explicit public dates or conservative text-resolved year hints; missing years are not backfilled.
Years inferred from description text are marked separately from explicit upload dates so time claims stay auditable.

feature_manifest.label: Duration (minutes)

feature_manifest.label: Channel reach (subscribers)
feature_manifest.description: How many subscribers the uploading channel has - a proxy for built-in reach.

feature_manifest.label: Virality (views per subscriber)
feature_manifest.description: Views divided by subscribers: how far a song travelled beyond its channel's base.

feature_manifest.label: Title length (words)
feature_manifest.description: Number of words in the video title.

feature_manifest.label: Title length (characters)
feature_manifest.description: Number of characters in the video title.

feature_manifest.label: Tag count
feature_manifest.description: How many YouTube tags the upload declared.

feature_manifest.label: Description length (characters)
feature_manifest.description: Character length of the video description.

feature_manifest.label: Official video
feature_manifest.description: 1 if the title/tags mark it an official video, else 0.

operating_model.name: Stream public data platform
operating_model.warehouse_shape: contract-checked raw, standardized, gold, and serving layers
serving_interfaces: static JSON exports for GitHub Pages
system_guards: quality gates on keys, nulls, and target leakage
gold metrics regenerated from the same processing code
clear separation between synthetic representation data and public music data

service_levels.name: Freshness posture
service_levels.current: Synthetic lane reruns on demand; public music refresh stays manual and declared.
service_levels.target: No published surface should imply a live feed when the source is bundled or manually refreshed.

service_levels.name: Contract coverage
service_levels.current: 2 contracted datasets with 10 visible quality gates.
service_levels.target: Every published lane ships with grain, key, partition, and quality posture before the chart layer.

service_levels.name: Delivery parity
service_levels.current: FastAPI, static JSON, and the browser all read from the same Python pipeline.
service_levels.target: Static hosting should not drift away from the live API or produce a second truth.

service_levels.name: Leakage discipline
service_levels.current: The music predictor excludes virality_coefficient because it is derived from views.
service_levels.target: No self-fulfilling feature should make the model look more clairvoyant than the data permits.

delivery_surfaces.name: Live API
delivery_surfaces.refresh: served from in-process caches; synthetic lane can be rerun per request

delivery_surfaces.name: Static export
delivery_surfaces.audience: GitHub Pages, preview deploys, no-backend readers
delivery_surfaces.refresh: rebuilt intentionally so public artifacts do not pretend to be live

delivery_surfaces.name: Browser surface
delivery_surfaces.refresh: reads API when present, otherwise falls back to baked artifacts

stages.name: Bronze landing
stages.purpose: Land raw public CSVs and synthetic generation seeds without pretending they are already analytic truth.

stages.name: Silver standardization
stages.purpose: Normalize titles, coerce numeric fields, enrich dates/languages/countries, and keep row-level grain intact.

stages.name: Gold semantics
stages.purpose: Aggregate stable metrics, inequality curves, effect sizes, archetypes, and scorecards that decision-makers can actually use.

stages.name: Serving contracts
stages.purpose: Expose the same gold outputs to FastAPI, static JSON, and the browser surface with the claim boundary still visible.

contracts.grain: one row per synthetic character per analysis run
contracts.update_mode: recomputed on demand through /api/analyze and cached in-process
contracts.freshness_sla: fresh per analysis run; static build snapshots are regenerated by build_static.py

quality_checks.name: Primary key uniqueness
quality_checks.detail: Character ids must stay unique inside a run.

quality_checks.name: Year partition range
quality_checks.detail: Synthetic cohort stays within the modeled platform era.

quality_checks.name: Non-negative measures
quality_checks.detail: Screen time and dialogue counts should never go below zero.

quality_checks.name: Allowed platform domain
quality_checks.detail: Platform values should remain inside the documented synthetic lane.

quality_checks.name: Null pressure
quality_checks.detail: The synthetic fact table is designed to ship fully populated rows.

quality_checks.name: Distinct title coverage
quality_checks.detail: Normalized-title deduplication should keep repeated songs from inflating counts.

quality_checks.detail: View count is the core analytic measure and must be present after cleaning.

quality_checks.name: Duration completeness
quality_checks.detail: Duration is allowed to be median-imputed, but the working fact table should not expose nulls.

quality_checks.name: Publication-year coverage
quality_checks.detail: Timeline charts only use rows with public upload dates.

quality_checks.name: Predictor hygiene
quality_checks.detail: The predictor set blocks virality_coefficient because it leaks the target.

quality_highlights.name: Claim boundary
quality_highlights.detail: Synthetic representation data and public music data stay separated all the way to the UI so the repo never pretends one lane proves the other.

quality_highlights.name: Target leakage block
quality_highlights.detail: Music prediction excludes virality_coefficient because it is derived from views and would make the model look smarter than it is.

quality_highlights.name: Gold parity
quality_highlights.detail: The static-host JSON exports are baked from the same Python pipeline the live API uses instead of from hand-edited files.

quality_highlights.name: Refresh discipline
quality_highlights.detail: The music lane exposes manual refresh status explicitly; the synthetic lane regenerates only when a new run is requested.

hero.headline: Public analysis should be able to survive a second question.
hero.subheadline: The same backend now computes the music lane, representation lane, runtime summaries, and front-end state.

signals.label: Representation parity
signals.note: Share balance in the active representation surface.

signals.label: Representation breadth
signals.note: Synthetic lane diversity index, computed in Python and exported for the UI.

signals.label: Attention concentration
signals.note: Top-3 channel control share in the public music lane.

signals.label: Notation-linked coverage
signals.note: Share of catalog songs with directly linked score or notation support.

researcher.title: Research lane
researcher.summary: Use the synthetic lane to test method, drift, and category structure before claiming anything about a private catalog.

engineer.title: Engineering lane
engineer.summary: The representation surface is structured for reproducibility: deterministic seeds, explicit exports, and inspectable metrics.

buyer.title: Buyer lane
buyer.summary: The public surface is strongest when it helps a team see where a narrow field is being mistaken for a neutral one.

artist.title: Artist read

summary.title: Artist lens
summary.summary: This is a market-shape read, not a songwriting oracle. In the classified slice, Electronic/Dance carries the strongest share of visible attention, the strongest duration pocket is 3–4 min, and collaboration helps most when it expands reach without making the track anonymous.
summary.takeaway: The practical question is not which genre is supposedly 'best.' It is whether your release shape can still find oxygen once attention narrows around a few familiar patterns.

artist.stakes: Exposure, collaboration timing, release shape, and category crowding.

consumer.title: Consumer read

summary.title: Consumer lens
summary.summary: Listeners are moving through a field that is both concentrated and partially labeled. The visible publication window spans 2009 to 2025, but only 26% of rows carry public year data, which means breadth has to be read with some caution.
summary.takeaway: A healthy discovery system should feel broader than its hit list. If the surface feels repetitive, the numbers suggest you may not be imagining it.

consumer.stakes: Discovery quality, variety, and whether recommendation loops keep collapsing back to the same center.

business.title: Business read

summary.title: Business lens
summary.summary: Attention remains narrow enough that a small set of channels can still steer the visible market. The top 5 hold 12.5% of views and the top 10 hold 19.1%, which is useful if you need to separate repeatable distribution logic from one-off mythology.
summary.takeaway: The value is not 'which song went viral.' It is which conditions keep narrowing or opening the market across multiple cohorts.

business.stakes: Channel concentration, inventory risk, promotional efficiency, and repeatable lift.

research.title: Research read

summary.title: Research lens
summary.summary: The useful question is not whether a pattern exists somewhere, but whether it survives classification gaps, coverage limits, and cohort changes. This lane is strongest when it keeps the leaderboard separate from the broader structural context instead of pretending they are the same thing.
summary.takeaway: The method earns trust when the story gets smaller where coverage is weak, and sharper where multiple checks agree.

research.stakes: Cohort integrity, feature leakage, and longitudinal comparability.

narratives.intro: The backend is now responsible for the repo's analytical posture: what is fresh, what is public, what is synthetic, and what still needs caution.

representation.summary: Representation findings stay small enough to inspect and specific enough to challenge.

music.summary: The public music lane stays separate so virality, concentration, and boost dynamics can be read on their own terms.

synthetic_vs_public.note: One lane asks how broad the field looks; the other asks how tightly attention concentrates.
Parity is not the same question as market control, but both reveal where the center hardens.
The music lane can now report how much of the catalog is connected to score-level evidence.

summary.promise: Each visual should answer a concrete question, name its boundary, and suggest how to improve the analysis instead of simply asking to be believed.
honesty_contract: Public visuals should stay traceable to row-level or export-level evidence.
If coverage is thin, confidence should fall in plain view.
If a chart is visually strong but analytically weak, the repo should say so directly.
The system should translate what changes when the signal moves, not just that it moved.

dashFieldMap.title: Multi-dimensional bias field
dashFieldMap.purpose: Show whether imbalance is broad across the field or concentrated inside a smaller platform-genre pocket. This is the first orientation surface, not the final verdict.
dashFieldMap.learn: The current synthetic run tracks parity at 0.74 and diversity at 0.71. The bubble field matters because a healthy average can still hide a small cluster where power keeps narrowing.
dashFieldMap.if_changes: If the upper-right cluster collapses when you change year, genre, or media type, the story is fragile. If it survives those slices, the signal is stronger and more portable.
dashFieldMap.improve: Improve this read by increasing sample size, checking the row explorer, and confirming whether the same cluster holds in the network, timeline, and role pattern surfaces.
dashFieldMap.limits: This view is synthetic and descriptive. It can show where to ask harder questions, but it cannot claim a real platform changed behavior without a real catalog lane.
dashFieldMap.scale: For larger companies, treat this as a triage surface: which segment deserves audit time, budget, or human review first.

dashLandscape.title: 3D representation landscape
dashLandscape.purpose: Stress-test the earlier field map in one extra dimension so a point that looked healthy in 2D can be challenged once another axis is visible.
dashLandscape.learn: This view is here to catch summary optimism. If a point keeps its position after a third axis is introduced, the signal is harder to dismiss as a charting artifact.
dashLandscape.if_changes: If the depth layer scrambles the earlier winners, your earlier explanation was under-specified. If the clusters stay coherent, the original story is more structurally sound.
dashLandscape.improve: Pair this with effect sizes and the decision grade. Three dimensions are useful, but they still need quality gates and confidence language.
dashLandscape.limits: 3D is powerful for pattern separation but weaker for exact reading. The value is shape, not precision-by-eye.

exploreRows.title: Row explorer

exploreMedia.title: Media-format comparison

exploreMedia.learn: This view exists to answer a practical question: does the pattern still hold once the media format changes, or was the original signal just hitching a ride on one production format?
exploreMedia.if_changes: If the signal disappears when media type changes, the earlier claim needs to narrow. If it stays stable, the pattern is more portable.
exploreMedia.improve: Improve this lane with clearer cohort sizes, stronger notes about edge cases, and side-by-side confidence language for thin slices.
exploreMedia.limits: Format labels are useful but incomplete. They should guide follow-up analysis, not stand in for it.
exploreMedia.scale: For larger teams, this helps explain whether a problem belongs to the whole system or just one format lane.

exploreGenre.title: Genre-specific pressure
exploreGenre.purpose: Show where a broad claim starts to splinter once genre enters, because many fairness or virality stories turn out to be genre-specific rather than universal.
exploreGenre.learn: The value here is not only the averages. It is seeing whether one genre keeps absorbing the same advantage while other lanes behave very differently.
exploreGenre.if_changes: If a result flips direction across genres, the repo should stop speaking in global terms and get more exact.
exploreGenre.improve: Improve this with better genre coverage notes, more year-aware comparisons, and clearer handling of miscellaneous or unclassified rows.
exploreGenre.limits: Genre labels are noisy and culturally loaded. They are a useful organizing surface, not a perfect ontology.
exploreGenre.scale: This becomes a budgeting and programming surface when teams need to know which lanes are actually driving the issue.

insightsSignals.title: Generated findings layer
insightsSignals.purpose: Translate the analytical field into readable conclusions without severing those conclusions from their evidence.
insightsSignals.learn: These generated findings matter only when they stay traceable to filters, cohorts, and metrics. Their job is to shorten time-to-understanding, not replace inspection.
insightsSignals.if_changes: If the generated wording changes dramatically under a small slice adjustment, the finding is unstable and should be presented more cautiously.
insightsSignals.improve: Keep attaching confidence, sample shape, and contradiction checks so the writing stays honest instead of polished.
insightsSignals.limits: Plain-language summaries are convenient, but they can over-smooth complexity if they are not constantly tied back to structure.
insightsSignals.scale: For larger companies, this is the handoff layer between deep analysis and people who need to act on it quickly.

libraryPropagationStudio.title: Bias propagation studio
libraryPropagationStudio.purpose: Show how a pattern becomes a system: where a small skew enters, how it compounds, and why downstream surfaces can end up looking natural even when the starting point was not.
libraryPropagationStudio.learn: This is the explanatory spine for people who can sense that something feels off but need to see the chain from input to consequence.
libraryPropagationStudio.if_changes: If one propagation step dominates the whole story, that is the intervention point. If many small steps compound, the system needs layered correction rather than one patch.
libraryPropagationStudio.improve: Improve it by adding more counterfactual examples and clearer notes about where the repo is modeling versus directly observing.
libraryPropagationStudio.limits: Propagation diagrams can look more certain than they are. They should declare where the evidence is direct and where it is inferential.
libraryPropagationStudio.scale: This is useful for ethics, policy, and engineering audiences because it connects abstract harm to a tractable chain.

mvPowerLaw.title: Power-law attention tail
mvPowerLaw.purpose: Test whether attention is being distributed broadly or whether a tiny winner tail is doing most of the work.
mvPowerLaw.learn: The real music lane covers 100 songs and 10,591,031,907 views. The power-law fit matters because virality claims usually sound organic long after the field has already become tail-dominated.
mvPowerLaw.if_changes: If the tail gets steeper, breakout concentration is easing. If it gets heavier, scale and incumbent reach are doing more of the outcome shaping.
mvPowerLaw.improve: Improve this with wider cohorts, better year segmentation, and side-by-side cohort comparisons so one leaderboard does not stand in for a market.
mvPowerLaw.limits: A heavy tail is descriptive, not accusatory. It tells you how concentrated attention looks, not why any individual song won.
mvPowerLaw.scale: This is where larger companies can see whether discovery infrastructure is broadening the field or mainly amplifying incumbent advantage.

mvInequality.title: Inequality of attention
mvInequality.purpose: Turn the winner-take-most feeling into measurable concentration so the conversation can move beyond impression.
mvInequality.learn: The current real-data Gini is 0.70 and top-10 share is 72%. That is the concrete shape of the field.
mvInequality.if_changes: If concentration drops while views remain strong, the market may be opening. If concentration rises, the field is becoming easier to describe but harder to enter.
mvInequality.improve: Keep cohort segmentation visible so inequality is not mistaken for one universal market law.
mvInequality.limits: Concentration metrics are blunt. They tell you where the center is, not what artistic or platform forces created it.
mvInequality.scale: Useful for strategy, procurement, and creator economics because it shows whether success is being distributed or hoarded.

mvCorrelation.title: Correlation architecture
mvCorrelation.purpose: Separate what travels with scale from what appears to matter on its own.
mvCorrelation.learn: Raw correlation is only the first pass. The partial layer matters because it tests whether a feature still holds once channel size stops carrying it.
mvCorrelation.if_changes: If a feature looks strong in the raw view but collapses in the partial view, scale was doing most of the talking.
mvCorrelation.improve: Improve this lane with more contrastive explanations, cleaner feature provenance, and clearer labeling of pre- versus post-publication fields.
mvCorrelation.limits: Correlation can suggest where to look harder, but it does not prove mechanism.
mvCorrelation.scale: This is the layer that keeps a music or media strategy conversation from drifting into superstition.

mvArchetypes.title: Viral archetypes
mvArchetypes.purpose: Turn a noisy leaderboard into a handful of reusable release shapes so readers can reason about strategies, not just songs.
mvArchetypes.learn: The clusters matter because they tell you whether success keeps arriving through the same small set of release patterns.
mvArchetypes.if_changes: If archetypes collapse into one blob when the feature mix changes, the segmentation is decorative. If they remain separable, it is carrying real structure.
mvArchetypes.improve: Keep naming the cluster assumptions, exposing cluster counts, and checking whether the groupings survive under different windows.
mvArchetypes.limits: Cluster names are interpretive. They help memory and discussion, but they should not be mistaken for immutable market categories.
mvArchetypes.scale: This is where product, label, and platform teams can start comparing release tactics without flattening everything into one average.

mvNetwork.title: Tag universe
mvNetwork.purpose: Map the neighboring language around music releases so the repo can show whether discovery is broadening or just recirculating a familiar cluster.
mvNetwork.learn: This surface is useful because it shows bridge tags, central tags, and isolated tag pockets that are easy to miss in a table.
mvNetwork.if_changes: If the same tags remain central under different cohorts, the field is more repetitive than its long tail suggests.
mvNetwork.improve: Improve it by keeping tag cleaning visible, adding time windows, and showing when a dense cluster is being carried by only a few channels.
mvNetwork.limits: Tags are packaging signals as much as musical signals, so they should be read with that commercial bias in mind.
mvNetwork.scale: For search, discovery, and editorial teams, this becomes a practical map of how the catalog is being framed.

mvPredictability.title: Predictability surface
mvPredictability.purpose: Force the repo to be honest about how much can actually be forecast before publication.
mvPredictability.learn: The current out-of-fold ensemble R² is 0.67. That matters because a low but honest score is more useful than a glamorous model with leakage.
mvPredictability.if_changes: If predictive lift rises with clean features, the field has legible structure. If it only rises when you leak post-publication data, the model is pretending.
mvPredictability.improve: Keep leakage blocks explicit, add calibration notes, and broaden the feature audit so contributors can improve the model without gaming the story.
mvPredictability.limits: Prediction is not explanation. Even a strong model does not tell you whether the mechanism is fair or healthy.
mvPredictability.scale: For larger teams this is the bridge between analytical curiosity and something operationally useful.

mvResonance.title: Resonance mapper
mvResonance.purpose: Give the repo a way to talk about repeated attention pressure without pretending it can observe hidden platform internals.
mvResonance.learn: This visual is useful because it makes the public shape of repetition legible: duration fit, release pressure, concentration, and tag/title echo can still reveal an environment optimized for recurrence.
mvResonance.if_changes: If resonance narrows and stabilizes around the same tracks, the field is leaning toward manufactured repeatability. If it diffuses, discovery may be opening up.
mvResonance.improve: Improve it with better cohort windows, more public context data, and clearer translation between repeat pressure and human consequence.
mvResonance.limits: This is a public proxy, not a claim about hidden recommender code or audio waveforms.
mvResonance.scale: This is where virality stops sounding mystical and starts looking environmental.

mvWhatIf.title: What-if predictor
mvWhatIf.purpose: Give readers a safe way to stress the public model and see how one feature change alters the rank story.
mvWhatIf.learn: This matters because most people need to feel the slope of the model before they understand what the summary metrics are saying.
mvWhatIf.if_changes: If a tiny control change produces a wild output swing, the model or feature space needs better calibration.
mvWhatIf.improve: Improve it with clearer uncertainty language, cohort-aware presets, and more visible notes about what the model does not know.
mvWhatIf.limits: A simulator is a directional instrument, not a guarantee of outcome.
mvWhatIf.scale: This is the most approachable bridge from research to practical planning because it lets non-specialists test consequences directly.

mvBias.title: Bias in the top 100
mvBias.purpose: Show that virality is not only a music question. It is also a distribution, exposure, and advantage question.
mvBias.learn: Publication year coverage is 19% and notation link rate is 0%. That honesty matters because missing context should lower confidence, not be paved over.
mvBias.if_changes: If gender, genre, or language concentration shifts under a new role lens, the conclusion should change in consequence, not in facts.
mvBias.improve: Keep growing country, year, and notation coverage so this section can move from structural hints to deeper musical and market evidence.
mvBias.limits: This lane uses public data and public inference. It should stay explicit about what is observed, what is classified, and what is still unknown.
mvBias.scale: For entertainment, platform, and creator teams, this is the section that translates abstract bias into market shape.

mv3DLandscape.title: 3D virality landscape
mv3DLandscape.purpose: Give the reader one higher-dimensional pass on views, virality, and channel scale at the same time so strategy does not get trapped in a flat ranking.
mv3DLandscape.learn: The point is not the spectacle. The point is to see whether breakout songs occupy genuinely different terrain or are just scaled-up versions of the same release pattern.
mv3DLandscape.if_changes: If the 3D shape shows clear separation, strategy families are more believable. If it turns into a compressed cloud, the market is behaving more uniformly than the labels suggest.
mv3DLandscape.improve: Improve it with stronger cohort filters and better explanatory labels so the depth dimension stays legible to non-specialists.
mv3DLandscape.limits: 3D scenes are intuitive for shape and weaker for precise reading. They need companion annotations.
mv3DLandscape.scale: This is useful in demos and reviews because it gives one quick sense of whether the field has multiple lanes or one narrow groove.

mvSongsExplorer.title: Public songs explorer
mvSongsExplorer.purpose: Keep the real-data lane falsifiable by letting readers inspect, sort, and challenge the rows beneath the story.
mvSongsExplorer.learn: The current score-aware lane has 0 discovered local music files and 0 matched catalog songs. That tells the reader how deep the note-aware bridge actually goes today.
mvSongsExplorer.if_changes: If the summary feels persuasive but the rows or note links look thin, the repo should say so plainly.
mvSongsExplorer.improve: Keep adding pagination, stronger schema cues, and more score-linked public files so contributors can build on a real substrate instead of a screenshot.
mvSongsExplorer.limits: An explorer is only useful if the repository keeps the row layer honest and updated.
mvSongsExplorer.scale: This is the collaborator surface: the place where people can verify, extend, and actually contribute.

mvArchetypes.scale: This is where product, label, and platform teams can start comparing release tactics without flattening everything into one average.

mvNetwork.title: Tag universe
mvNetwork.purpose: Map the neighboring language around music releases so the repo can show whether discovery is broadening or just recirculating a familiar cluster.
mvNetwork.learn: This surface is useful because it shows bridge tags, central tags, and isolated tag pockets that are easy to miss in a table.
mvNetwork.if_changes: If the same tags remain central under different cohorts, the field is more repetitive than its long tail suggests.
mvNetwork.improve: Improve it by keeping tag cleaning visible, adding time windows, and showing when a dense cluster is being carried by only a few channels.
mvNetwork.limits: Tags are packaging signals as much as musical signals, so they should be read with that commercial bias in mind.
mvNetwork.scale: For search, discovery, and editorial teams, this becomes a practical map of how the catalog is being framed.

mvPredictability.title: Predictability surface
mvPredictability.purpose: Force the repo to be honest about how much can actually be forecast before publication.
mvPredictability.learn: The current out-of-fold ensemble R² is 0.67. That matters because a low but honest score is more useful than a glamorous model with leakage.
mvPredictability.if_changes: If predictive lift rises with clean features, the field has legible structure. If it only rises when you leak post-publication data, the model is pretending.
mvPredictability.improve: Keep leakage blocks explicit, add calibration notes, and broaden the feature audit so contributors can improve the model without gaming the story.
mvPredictability.limits: Prediction is not explanation. Even a strong model does not tell you whether the mechanism is fair or healthy.
mvPredictability.scale: For larger teams this is the bridge between analytical curiosity and something operationally useful.

mvResonance.title: Resonance mapper
mvResonance.purpose: Give the repo a way to talk about repeated attention pressure without pretending it can observe hidden platform internals.
mvResonance.learn: This visual is useful because it makes the public shape of repetition legible: duration fit, release pressure, concentration, and tag/title echo can still reveal an environment optimized for recurrence.
mvResonance.if_changes: If resonance narrows and stabilizes around the same tracks, the field is leaning toward manufactured repeatability. If it diffuses, discovery may be opening up.
mvResonance.improve: Improve it with better cohort windows, more public context data, and clearer translation between repeat pressure and human consequence.
mvResonance.limits: This is a public proxy, not a claim about hidden recommender code or audio waveforms.
mvResonance.scale: This is where virality stops sounding mystical and starts looking environmental.

mvWhatIf.title: What-if predictor
mvWhatIf.purpose: Give readers a safe way to stress the public model and see how one feature change alters the rank story.
mvWhatIf.learn: This matters because most people need to feel the slope of the model before they understand what the summary metrics are saying.
mvWhatIf.if_changes: If a tiny control change produces a wild output swing, the model or feature space needs better calibration.
mvWhatIf.improve: Improve it with clearer uncertainty language, cohort-aware presets, and more visible notes about what the model does not know.
mvWhatIf.limits: A simulator is a directional instrument, not a guarantee of outcome.
mvWhatIf.scale: This is the most approachable bridge from research to practical planning because it lets non-specialists test consequences directly.

mvBias.title: Bias in the top 100
mvBias.purpose: Show that virality is not only a music question. It is also a distribution, exposure, and advantage question.
mvBias.learn: Publication year coverage is 19% and notation link rate is 0%. That honesty matters because missing context should lower confidence, not be paved over.
mvBias.if_changes: If gender, genre, or language concentration shifts under a new role lens, the conclusion should change in consequence, not in facts.
mvBias.improve: Keep growing country, year, and notation coverage so this section can move from structural hints to deeper musical and market evidence.
mvBias.limits: This lane uses public data and public inference. It should stay explicit about what is observed, what is classified, and what is still unknown.
mvBias.scale: For entertainment, platform, and creator teams, this is the section that translates abstract bias into market shape.

mv3DLandscape.title: 3D virality landscape
mv3DLandscape.purpose: Give the reader one higher-dimensional pass on views, virality, and channel scale at the same time so strategy does not get trapped in a flat ranking.
mv3DLandscape.learn: The point is not the spectacle. The point is to see whether breakout songs occupy genuinely different terrain or are just scaled-up versions of the same release pattern.
mv3DLandscape.if_changes: If the 3D shape shows clear separation, strategy families are more believable. If it turns into a compressed cloud, the market is behaving more uniformly than the labels suggest.
mv3DLandscape.improve: Improve it with stronger cohort filters and better explanatory labels so the depth dimension stays legible to non-specialists.
mv3DLandscape.limits: 3D scenes are intuitive for shape and weaker for precise reading. They need companion annotations.
mv3DLandscape.scale: This is useful in demos and reviews because it gives one quick sense of whether the field has multiple lanes or one narrow groove.

mvSongsExplorer.title: Public songs explorer
mvSongsExplorer.purpose: Keep the real-data lane falsifiable by letting readers inspect, sort, and challenge the rows beneath the story.
mvSongsExplorer.learn: The current score-aware lane has 0 discovered local music files and 0 matched catalog songs. That tells the reader how deep the note-aware bridge actually goes today.
mvSongsExplorer.if_changes: If the summary feels persuasive but the rows or note links look thin, the repo should say so plainly.
mvSongsExplorer.improve: Keep adding pagination, stronger schema cues, and more score-linked public files so contributors can build on a real substrate instead of a screenshot.
mvSongsExplorer.limits: An explorer is only useful if the repository keeps the row layer honest and updated.
mvSongsExplorer.scale: This is the collaborator surface: the place where people can verify, extend, and actually contribute.

notes: Synthetic and public lanes stay separated.
Static exports and API responses should originate from the same runtime package.
SQLite snapshots are for inspectable persistence, not for hiding logic.

core_top_100.purpose: Used for the core view, inequality, correlation, archetype, network, and prediction charts.

enriched_context.purpose: Used where broader structural checks matter: bias, language mix, publication timing, and resonance context.

source_audit.notes: Primary cohort used for the core charts and leaderboard surfaces.
Filtered to music videos before deduplication against the primary cohort.
Adds duration, engagement, subscriber, and publication timing context where available.

cleaning_steps: Coerce view counts to numeric and drop rows that still cannot be measured.
Normalize title strings before cross-source deduplication so the same song is not counted twice.
Convert durations to minutes and fill missing durations with the source median rather than inventing a default.
Fill missing subscriber counts with the source median so reach-adjusted comparisons remain stable.
Map channel countries only when a public lookup exists; otherwise keep the value as Unknown instead of guessing.
Carry publication years directly from public date fields, and only infer a year from free text when a single recent year or copyright marker is unambiguous.
Keep the top-100 cohort separate from the enriched context so broad structural claims do not overwrite the core leaderboard.
guardrails: Virality coefficient is excluded from the predictor set because it is derived from views and would leak the target.
All prediction scores are reported out-of-fold with 5-fold cross-validation, not in-sample fit.
Power-law confidence intervals use 500 bootstrap resamples over the fitted tail only.
Correlation rows include partial Spearman checks so raw associations are not mistaken for independent effects.
Publication timelines are shown only for rows with explicit public dates or conservative text-resolved year hints; missing years are not backfilled.
Years inferred from description text are marked separately from explicit upload dates so time claims stay auditable.

feature_manifest.label: Duration (minutes)
feature_manifest.description: Track length in minutes. The data peaks near the 3-minute radio-single norm.

feature_manifest.label: Channel reach (subscribers)
feature_manifest.description: How many subscribers the uploading channel has - a proxy for built-in reach.

feature_manifest.label: Virality (views per subscriber)
feature_manifest.description: Views divided by subscribers: how far a song travelled beyond its channel's base.

feature_manifest.label: Title length (words)
feature_manifest.description: Number of words in the video title.

feature_manifest.label: Title length (characters)
feature_manifest.description: Number of characters in the video title.

feature_manifest.label: Tag count
feature_manifest.description: How many YouTube tags the upload declared.

feature_manifest.label: Description length (characters)
feature_manifest.description: Character length of the video description.

feature_manifest.label: Official video
feature_manifest.description: 1 if the title/tags mark it an official video, else 0.

synthetic_vs_public.note: One lane asks how broad the field looks; the other asks how tightly attention concentrates.
Parity is not the same question as market control, but both reveal where the center hardens.
The music lane can now report how much of the catalog is connected to score-level evidence.
